\label{sec:conclusions}

We draw conclusions here for the association tests, the foreground census, and
the dispersion-budget decomposition; conclusions on the scattering fits, the
scintillation products, the turbulence parameters, and the burst energies follow
in the corresponding sections.

\begin{itemize}
\item \textit{Association.} All twelve candidate pairs are genuine
co-detections: each has a chance-coincidence probability $P_{\rm cc}<10^{-8}$
under conservative, chance-maximizing windows and timing residuals consistent with
the inter-site geometric delay under the shared DSA-DM reference convention.
Eight of the twelve additionally pass an independent CHIME--DSA DM-agreement
check; the other four rest on position and timing alone.

\item \textit{Foreground census.} The FRB~20230307A sightline is the only one in
the twelve-burst sample that passes within $R_{500}$ of a foreground cluster,
contributing an intracluster column of order $160\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$
(uncertain at the factor-of-two level, $\approx100$--$560\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$
across the profile choice and the cluster-parameter systematics).
The intervening circumgalactic and intracluster contribution to the dispersion
budget is therefore appreciable on that sightline alone and small on the rest;
the confirmed foreground galaxies lie in the outer circumgalactic medium, where
individual columns are minor. The census reaches only as deep as the wide-field
catalogs available at this declination, so a sightline without a confirmed
intervening system is unconstrained by survey depth, not demonstrably free of
foreground gas.

\item \textit{Dispersion budget.} With $\mathrm{DM_{host}}$ forward-modeled as a
posterior rather than an arithmetic residual, the decomposition leaves host
contributions that are a minority of the extragalactic column on every
redshift-constrained sightline (median $\mathrm{DM_{host}}$ from
$\approx10$ to $210\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$, with broad cosmology-dominated
uncertainties): the extragalactic column is dominated by the cosmological
term, not the host. The intervening term is concentrated on the single
cluster-crossing sightline, and the two highest-redshift sightlines are
consistent with zero host ($P(\mathrm{DM_{host}}<0)\approx0.45$), their
formerly negative point residuals reflecting the skew of the cosmological
distribution rather than an unphysical host. Sightlines without spectroscopic
host redshifts are excluded from all distance-dependent statements.
\end{itemize}

\label{app:assoc-cards}

This appendix collects the per-burst association-card figures referenced in the
main text. Each card is a compact, at-a-glance cross-check that the CHIME/FRB and
DSA-110 triggers correspond to the same astrophysical burst, and that the
association used throughout the manuscript is internally consistent.

Across the sample, the cards summarize (i) the timing consistency between the
two pipelines after accounting for barycentric corrections and dispersion, (ii)
sky-position consistency between the candidate localizations (to the extent
available for each burst), and (iii) dispersion-measure consistency and
bookkeeping (e.g., the CHIME/FRB-reported DM compared to any independently
reconstructed DM used in downstream analysis). Many cards also include catalog
context (nearby galaxies, foreground structures, or survey footprints) that is
used as a qualitative sanity check on the association and as a pointer to the
foreground-census layer described in the main text.

When reading the cards, the most important failure modes they are designed to
catch are: a mis-identified counterpart between instruments, a bookkeeping
mismatch (wrong TOA, wrong DM, or wrong metadata version), or an association
that is only explainable by implausible timing/DM offsets. We treat the cards as
validation artifacts: they motivate which bursts advance to the joint
DM/scattering analysis, but we avoid over-interpreting any individual panel
beyond confirming that the association inputs are coherent.

\section{Intracluster dispersion for foreground clusters}
\label{app:clusters}

This appendix collects the per-cluster hot-gas dispersion columns behind the
$b\le R_{500}$ budget cut described in the main text. Only the single cluster
that pierces the halo enters $\mathrm{DM_{int}}$; the remaining panels show the
excluded clusters and the near-miss that motivate the cut being a principled
impact-parameter threshold rather than a hand-picked selection.

\begin{figure*}
    \centering
    \includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{figures/clusters_icm.pdf}
    \caption{Predicted intracluster hot-gas dispersion for the four foreground
    clusters along the FRB~20230307A sightline. Each panel plots the
    modified-NFW hot-baryon dispersion column $\mathrm{DM}(b)$ against projected
    impact parameter $b$ (blue); the vertical marker is the sightline's measured
    impact parameter, the dashed line is $R_{500}$, and the shaded region is
    inside $R_{500}$. Only J115120.4$+$714435 ($b/R_{500}=0.83$) meets the
    $b\le R_{500}$ budget cut and enters $\mathrm{DM_{int}}$
    ($\sim$160\,pc\,cm$^{-3}$ from this mNFW profile, factor-of-two uncertain across
    profile and cluster-parameter systematics,
    Appendix~\ref{app:host-forward-model}; Table~\ref{tab:foreground}); the
    others lie beyond
    $R_{500}$ and are excluded even where the profile remains formally nonzero
    (J115128.2$+$713637 at $b/R_{500}=1.25$, $\sim$95\,pc\,cm$^{-3}$). Cluster
    masses ($\log M_{500}$) and radii are from the \citet{WenHan2024} DESI
    Legacy/WISE catalog.}
    \label{fig:clusters_icm}
\end{figure*}

\clearpage
\FloatBarrier

\section{Forward-modeled host dispersion}
\label{app:host-forward-model}

The host term of Table~\ref{tab:budget} is a forward-modeled posterior rather
than an arithmetic residual (Section~\ref{sec:dm}). For each redshift-constrained
sightline we draw $N=2\times10^{5}$ Monte-Carlo samples of
$\mathrm{DM_{host}}=\mathrm{DM_{obs}}-\mathrm{DM_{MW,ISM}}-\mathrm{DM_{MW,halo}}
-\mathrm{DM_{cosmic}}-\mathrm{DM_{int}}$, propagating priors on the non-host
terms:
\begin{itemize}
\item \emph{Cosmological.} $\mathrm{DM_{cosmic}}=\Delta\,\langle\mathrm{DM_{cosmic}}
\rangle(z)$ with $\Delta$ from the \citet{Macquart2020} probability density
$p(\Delta)\propto\Delta^{-\beta'}\exp[-(\Delta^{-\alpha'}-C_0)^2/(2\alpha'^2
\sigma_{\rm DM}^2)]$, $\alpha'=\beta'=3$, $\sigma_{\rm DM}=F\,z^{-1/2}$, and $F$
uniform on $[0.25,0.40]$ \citep{McQuinn2014, James2022}; $C_0$ is fixed so
$\langle\Delta\rangle=1$.
\item \emph{Galactic disk.} The NE2025 disk column with a $30\%$ lognormal
uncertainty.
\item \emph{Galactic halo.} $40\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ median, lognormal with a
factor-$\sim\!2$ span ($2\sigma$ range $\approx[20,80]\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$;
\citealt{YamasakiTotani2020, KeatingPen2020, Cook2023}).
\item \emph{Intervening.} The Table~\ref{tab:foreground} column with a $40\%$
(measured-mass halo), factor-$2$ (assumed-mass halo), or $30\%$ (cluster)
lognormal uncertainty.
\end{itemize}
The resulting posteriors are broad and asymmetric, dominated at higher redshift
by the cosmological scatter (Figure~\ref{fig:dm_host_posteriors},
Table~\ref{tab:host-forward-model}); their medians exceed the mean-subtracted
residuals because $P(\mathrm{DM_{cosmic}})$ is right-skewed. The prior widths
above are fiducial and are documented so they can be revised; the analysis and
both artifacts are reproduced by \texttt{scripts/dm\_budget\_uncertainty.py}.

\tablecaption{Forward-modeled host-dispersion posteriors for the nine
redshift-constrained sightlines: median with $16$th--$84$th-percentile interval,
and $P(\mathrm{DM_{host}}<0)$. DM in $\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$.
\label{tab:host-forward-model}}
\tablehead{\colhead{Burst} & \colhead{$z$} & \colhead{$\mathrm{DM_{host}}$} &
  \colhead{$P(\mathrm{DM_{host}}<0)$}}

\begin{figure*}
    \centering
    \includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{figures/dm_host_posteriors.pdf}
    \caption{\emph{Left:} forward-modeled host-dispersion posteriors (median and
    $16$th--$84$th-percentile interval, dark points) for the nine
    redshift-constrained sightlines, compared with the arithmetic
    mean-subtracted residual (orange crosses). Sampling the full, skewed
    $P(\mathrm{DM_{cosmic}}\,|\,z)$ shifts the posterior median above the
    mean-subtracted value and turns the two formerly negative residuals
    (FRB 20220310F, FRB 20221203A) into posteriors consistent with zero.
    \emph{Right:} the FRB 20230307A intracluster column from an X-ray-motivated
    isothermal $\beta$-model Monte Carlo (over $M_{500}$, $f_{\rm gas}$, and
    profile shape); the vertical line marks the two-phase mNFW value
    ($\sim\!160\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$) adopted in the budget.}
    \label{fig:dm_host_posteriors}
\end{figure*}

\clearpage
\FloatBarrier

\section{Joint-model morphology audits}
\label{app:jointmodel-pairs}

This appendix collects the per-burst two-dimensional data/model/residual panels
for the current joint-model morphology audit. The panels are included to make
the component-multiplicity and residual checks visible in the manuscript, while
leaving the fit-derived turbulence and scattering parameters deferred to the
completed scattering analysis.
